questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Swadeshi' movement with its main features.
questions.answer: The Swadeshi movement was a form of non-cooperation movement that emerged in India in response to the partition of Bengal. Its main features include the boycott of British goods, establishment of national industries and the promotion of Swadeshi goods.

questions.q: Who is the author of the book 'The Discovery of India'?
What was the main objective of the Champaran Satyagraha?
questions.answer: The main objective of the Champaran Satyagraha was to raise the issue of indigo cultivation and the exploitation of peasants by European planters.

questions.q: Fill in the blanks: The main feature of the Quit India Movement was the call for _______________.
Explain the significance of the Dandi March.
questions.answer: The Dandi March was a significant event in the Indian independence movement as it marked a major phase of civil disobedience against the British colonial rule.

questions.q: State with reasons why the Indian National Congress was banned in 1942.
questions.answer: The Indian National Congress was banned in 1942 because of the Quit India Movement. The movement was seen as a threat to the British colonial rule and the Congress was therefore banned.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920-22?
Write a note on the role of Tilak in the Indian National Movement.
questions.answer: Tilak played a significant role in the Indian National Movement by advocating for swaraj and by organizing the Swadeshi movement.

questions.q: Explain the terms 'Jawaharlal Nehru and Gandhi' in relation to the Quit India Movement.
questions.answer: Jawaharlal Nehru and Gandhi were the key leaders of the Quit India Movement. Nehru was imprisoned in Ahmednagar and Gandhi was arrested in Bombay.

questions.q: Who is the author of the book 'Gandhi and Anarchy'?
